{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 7.8, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.3, "key_event": "Nehruvian state-led industrialization and permit-license Raj expansion", "reasoning": "State capacity grew markedly through planned industrialization, nationalizations (SBI, LIC), the Hindu Code Bills, and administrative/coercive reach (President's Rule in Kerala), though the 1962 Sino-Indian defeat capped the gain. Society held steady: democratic contestation and successful linguistic mobilization (States Reorganisation) empowered it, but Congress one-party co-optation, caste \"cages of norms,\" and elite-captured land reform offset those gains."}
